hero.label: java / js / APIs
hero.title: plugins, bots and bespoke systems
hero.subtitle: I build Paper and Spigot plugins, Discord bots, and whatever odd jobs come my way. No template reselling — every line written from scratch.

services.label: services
services.title: What I build
services.plugins_desc: Paper and Spigot plugins. Commands, internal systems, weird mechanics — as long as it compiles.
services.bots_desc: Discord bots with Discord.js or JDA. Tickets, moderation, automation, the usual.
services.writing_desc: I write when it comes to me. Not a service, just here because I'm human.
services.consulting_desc: I help you define scope, choose tech, and avoid screw-ups before starting a project.

about.label: profile
about.title: Technical profile
about.whoami: A dev who builds stuff for Minecraft and Discord.
about.stack: java/ paper-api/ spigot/ javascript/ node/ discord.js/ html-css/

projects.title: Projects
projects.description: Stuff I've made. Some public, some private — whatever folks have paid me to build.
projects.discord_desc: Bots and automation for private servers.
projects.timetofly_desc: A plugin that grants timed flight based on time or permissions. Nothing complex, but nicely configurable.
projects.timetofly_f1: Per-group or per-player control via LuckPerms.
projects.bestsupplies_desc: Configurable automatic kits and deliveries by event, time or permissions. Open source, in case someone wants to tinker.
projects.airwavechat_desc: An experiment that ended up working: separate chat channels with custom formatting. So the global channel doesn't get flooded.
projects.discord_bot_name: Private stack for a Discord community
projects.discord_bot_desc: Two private bots handling internal management, activities, points and anonymous submissions.
projects.bot_events_desc: Internal dynamics, events and mini-games to keep the community active.
projects.bot_submissions_desc: Anonymous content submission with review queue and auto-publishing.
projects.cta_desc: DM me on Discord and we'll see what we can do.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: GitHub Pages, no backend. No forms or personal data published here.
contact.line3_out: I don't publish email, location or hours on the site.
contact.discord_desc: For support, proposals or technical questions.
contact.card_what_2: Discord bots with tickets, mini-games or automation.
contact.card_tips_1: Tell me what you need, which platform and when.
contact.card_tips_2: If there's existing code, share the repo or context.
contact.card_tips_3: If it's support: version, error, how to reproduce.
contact.card_tips_note: If it's a private project, better come with clear requirements.

poetry.title: Poetry
poetry.description: Writing on the side. Nothing to sell here.

legal.title: Legal
legal.description: Legal stuff in one page. No fluff.
legal.privacy_p1: This site is fully static (GitHub Pages, no backend). No cookies, no tracking, no analytics, no forms that send data. The only data stored in your browser is theme preference (light/dark) via localStorage, and it is not transmitted anywhere.
legal.privacy_p2: GitHub's servers may log IP addresses and HTTP headers per their own privacy policy. If you contact me via Discord, any data you share is subject to Discord's privacy policy.
legal.license_p1: Software I publish as open source (plugins, bots, libraries) is under the MIT license unless the repository states otherwise. You may use, modify and distribute it freely, as long as you keep the copyright notice and original license.
legal.license_p2: Private or custom-developed software for clients is governed by the terms agreed in each contract. Redistribution without express permission is not allowed.
legal.terms_scope: Scope: Defined in a written proposal before starting. Subsequent changes may adjust pricing and deadlines.
legal.terms_payment: Payment: Agreed per project. Typically 50% upfront and 50% on delivery, unless otherwise agreed.
legal.terms_ip: Intellectual property: The code belongs to you once paid. I reserve the right to reuse generic patterns or components.
legal.terms_warranty: Warranty: 30 days for bugs reported after delivery. Does not cover your modifications or third-party issues.
legal.terms_support: Support: The level of support is defined per agreement. For free or open source projects, it is best-effort with no guaranteed response time.
legal.disclaimer_text: All free and open source software is provided "as is", without warranty of any kind. I am not liable for damages, data loss or any other issues arising from its use.
